# Ordered Oils fix queue as of 2026-04-04.
# Source of truth: current `tests/fixtures/oils/pass-list.txt` plus `tests/oils_spec.rs`
# skip rules and `tests/common/oils_format.rs` case parsing.
# Current backlog below: 132 xfails across 21 files, with 42 whole-file skips in `tests/oils_spec.rs`.
#
# ── How the Oils test infra works ──
# Runner:      tests/oils_spec.rs (datatest_stable harness, discovers .test.sh files)
# Format:      tests/common/oils_format.rs parses the Oils .test.sh format into cases
# Pass-list:   tests/fixtures/oils/pass-list.txt — one "file:case name" per line.
#              Everything IN the list must pass; everything NOT in it defaults to xfail.
#              Unexpected passes and regressions are both hard failures.
# Regenerate:  OILS_GENERATE_PASS_LIST=1 cargo test --test oils_spec 2>/dev/null \
#                | grep '^PASS_LIST:' | sed 's/^PASS_LIST://' | sort > pass-list.txt
#              ⚠ Run multi-threaded (default); single-threaded gives fewer passes due to
#              pre-existing test-isolation flakiness. Take the union of 3 runs for stability.
# Run one file: cargo test --test oils_spec -- "assign-extended" --nocapture --test-threads=1
# Verbose xfails: OILS_VERBOSE_XFAIL=1 cargo test --test oils_spec -- "file" --nocapture
# Skip list:   skip_files() in oils_spec.rs — whole-file skips for non-applicable tests.
# Override precedence in .test.sh format: N-I > BUG > OK > default (per-shell).
#
# ── Backlog ──
# This is a file-level backlog grouped by the current suspected blocker.
# Each line is one Oils `.test.sh` file with remaining xfails.
# Permanent whole-file skips and complete files are intentionally excluded.
# `nul-bytes.test` and `unicode.test` still map to Milestone 9 fidelity work.


## Fully local (67 xfails / 16 files)
- sh-usage.test (xfail=2) `sh` / `bash` invocation and locale handling
- bugs.test (xfail=4) mixed regression bucket; remaining: NUL byte, /dev/zero, arith code injection (x2)
- redirect.test (xfail=4) redirection semantics; remaining: mkfifo, `{var}` parsing edge cases, fd leak, xtrace routing
- builtin-cd.test (xfail=7) `cd` / `pwd` symlink fidelity (M9/VFS)
- glob.test (xfail=3) command-as-glob, escaped `-` in charclass, LC_COLLATE
- globignore.test (xfail=3) subdirectory patterns (x2), charclass `[[:alnum:]]`
- process-sub.test (xfail=2) standalone and redirected `>()` plumbing
- pipeline.test (xfail=1) SIGPIPE (exit 141 = 128+13)
- nul-bytes.test (xfail=9) binary/NUL semantics (M9 binary fidelity)
- unicode.test (xfail=6) Unicode escape / invalid-codepoint fidelity (M9 text fidelity)
- array-sparse.test (xfail=2) compgen -F, non-numeric keys in indexed init
- var-ref.test (xfail=12) indirect expansion: subscripts, suffix ops, `@Q`, code injection, tilde
- array.test (xfail=4) env-binding rejection, space-before-`(` behavior, multiple subscripts
- nameref.test (xfail=3) `a[expr]`, `a[@]`, and invalid mutation through nameref
- array-assoc.test (xfail=4) slicing/quoting, assoc reset semantics, brace-expansion interaction, `${a[@]@Q}`
- introspect.test (xfail=1) `BASH_LINENO` / `BASH_SOURCE` off-by-2 in sourced files

## Local but parser-adjacent (65 xfails / 6 files)
- extglob-match.test (xfail=1) `!(str)` in `[[ ]]` changes meaning after `shopt -s extglob`
- alias.test (xfail=26) move alias expansion before parse; replace the post-AST `split_whitespace()` hack
- parse-errors.test (xfail=6) `${%}`, `}` on second line, `sh -i -c` parse error mapping, array literal inside array, and long-token cases currently blocked by external-command (`python2`) unavailability in VFS
- command-sub.test (xfail=15) backtick quoting and newline semantics; likely needs local rewrite / normalization before parse
- regex.test (xfail=7) `[[ =~ ]]` spacing/operator parsing plus quoted char-class fidelity (1 case may still need upstream regex-lexer investigation)
- array-assign.test (xfail=10) spaces and nesting in LHS subscripts, plus context-sensitive array assignment parsing

## Need upstream changes (0 xfails / 0 files)
- None currently. Revisit this section if any remaining row stops looking locally fixable.
